text.title: rust-testing-property Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the rust-testing-property methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Rust crates containing parsers, codecs, serialization, or pure math MUST have at least one proptest! block covering the primary invariant.
rule.rationale: Hand-written tables cover the author's mental model; proptest catches the cases the author did not think of.

rule.statement: Record and commit failing proptest seeds. Configure FailurePersistence::WithSource("regression") so seeds are checked into git.
rule.rationale: A seed that reproduced a bug is a regression test for free.

rule.statement: Never use assert_eq! to compare f64 values. Use approx::assert_relative_eq! or assert_abs_diff_eq!.
rule.rationale: assert_eq! on f64 fails on representation differences like 0.1 + 0.2 != 0.3.

rule.statement: Cap cases at 256-1024 per property in CI to keep wall-clock cost bounded.
rule.rationale: Unbounded cases inflate CI minutes without proportional coverage gain.

rule.statement: Use prop_assert!/prop_assert_eq! inside proptest! blocks. Bare assert! produces opaque panic messages without the shrunk counterexample.
rule.rationale: proptest macros annotate the failing input on shrink output.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
